Resolves #14660

Ships line D2 ‚Äî the reveal/dismiss interaction contract ‚Äî on top of the D1 rail (stacked on `agent/14654-autohide-rail-affordance`, PR #14906; rebase onto `dev` + retarget follows its merge). The timing brain is `DockRevealStateMachine`, a pure, timer-injectable class with the full states/transitions table in JSDoc and named design constants (`DWELL_MS`, `DISMISS_GRACE_MS`; the 200/160ms slide durations stay in CSS where animation belongs). `DockRail` upgrades click from the D1 interim (direct restore) to the contract default ‚Äî click = focused transient reveal, re-click dismisses ‚Äî with hover-reveal strictly behind the workspace opt-in (`autoHideRevealOnHover`, dwell-gated, never steals focus), threaded through the adapter from projection options. `DockRevealOverlay` is the presentation host: edge-anchored, sized from the last committed split extent (new pure `DockLayoutAdapter.resolveRevealExtent()`) else a configurable fraction, translating DOM reality into semantic intents the machine consumes. The persist path is the overlay pin: `setItemPinned(true)` through the same reducer contract as every dock affordance ‚Äî dismissal in every form emits nothing.

Evidence: L2 (unit contract specs ‚Äî 190/190 dashboard suite green, incl. 13 machine specs on injected fake timers) ‚Üí L2 required (close-target ACs are spec-checkable: state table, focus-hold, executor-routed pin, never-persist). Residual: none ‚Äî live hover/focus DOM verification rides the Demo-A showcase + the reserved tour e2e (#14591).

## AC map

- **State machine documented; grace constants named** ‚Üí `DockRevealStateMachine` JSDoc carries the states table and the transitions table; `DWELL_MS` / `DISMISS_GRACE_MS` are named statics, workspace-overridable via rail configs (`revealDwellMs`, `revealDismissGraceMs`).
- **Focused pane never auto-dismisses** ‚Üí focus-hold specs at BOTH altitudes: machine (`focus-hold: a focused reveal never auto-dismisses`) and rail‚Üîoverlay binding (`bindRevealOverlay ‚Ä¶ focus-hold holds`).
- **Pin escape = executor op, no parallel path** ‚Üí `onRevealPinRequested` ‚Üí `commitOperation({operation: 'setItemPinned', pinned: true})` ‚Üí callback / `DockZoneModel.applyOperation()`; the model clears `autoHidden` itself (landed guard). Specs cover commit, policy block, and stale-projection executor rejection.
- **Reveal state never persists** ‚Üí dedicated spec: a full reveal/dismiss cycle leaves the document byte-identical and fires zero document-change notifications.
- **Cross-family review** ‚Üí requested after the stack rebases onto `dev` (per stacked-PR review semantics).

## Deltas from ticket

- **ADR precedence applied to the ticket's hover framing**: the ticket headlines "hover-reveal", but its own cited authority (the docking design record's interaction table) mandates click-reveal as DEFAULT with hover as an a11y-gated workspace opt-in. Both paths shipped; the default is click. The Demo-A S3 hover choreography runs on the opt-in config (flagged to the screenplay owner via A2A at intake).
- **D1's disabled-tab treatment migrates to the overlay pin control**: composing D1+D2 revealed that a `pinnable: false` item with a disabled tab would become unreachable content ‚Äî item loss. Reveal is policy-free; only PIN is policy-gated. The D1 tab-disable code and specs are superseded in this stack.
- **Pointer-away grace applies to unfocused reveals only** ‚Äî focused reveals dismiss on focus-leave/Escape/outside-click per the contract; this is the focus-hold rule, made explicit in the state table.
- **The pane-side collapse control** (tuck a visible pane to its rail) is out of scope here ‚Äî it lives with the pin-controls line (D4), not the rail's interaction layer.
- **Pane hosting is a documented slot contract** (`<overlay id>__pane-slot`): the consuming workspace mounts/clears the revealed pane, same seam as `resolveComponentRef` ‚Äî keeps the overlay pane-blind at this altitude.

## Test Evidence

```
npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/dashboard/
190 passed (30.9s)
```

New: `DockRevealStateMachine.spec.mjs` (13 ‚Äî dwell, grace, focus-hold, retarget, fail-close, destroy, all on injected fake timers), `DockRevealOverlay.spec.mjs` (5 ‚Äî visibility incl. grace window, sizing, pin policy mirror, intent translation). Rewritten: `DockRail.spec.mjs` (10 ‚Äî click-reveal, never-persist, pin escape, policy, hover opt-in, overlay binding, fail-close). Extended: `DockLayoutAdapter.spec.mjs` (`resolveRevealExtent` cases + hover opt-in threading).

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Rebase onto `dev` + retarget after PR #14906 merges; full CI on the merge candidate before cross-family approval.
- [ ] Demo-A scene-3 choreography drives reveal/dismiss live (screenplay sets `autoHideRevealOnHover` for the S3 hover beat).
- [ ] Tour e2e asserts reveal motion + focus-hold via `observe_motion` (#14591, reserved lane).

Closing per operator architecture challenge on the base PR: the stack assumed PR #14906's presentation shape was right, and it is not (raw vdom buttons + manual DOM delegation inside component.Base instead of real button.Base children / container composition / createVdomReference). The D2 logic layer survives untouched ‚Äî DockRevealStateMachine + its 13 specs, the adapter extent resolver, and the executor-routed pin contract re-land in a fresh PR from dev after the reworked #14906 merges. Ticket #14660 stays open and assigned. Branch retained for salvage.
